newsSinceJun6: June 9: QCOM -8.2% to ~$200 on news ByteDance is advancing a custom AI ASIC partnership with Qualcomm. The engagement itself is strategically positive (datacenter custom silicon beyond handsets; Amon had flagged a 'leading hyperscaler custom silicon engagement' shipping this calendar year), but the market sold it on export-control risk tied to a Chinese customer, sell-the-news profit-taking, and a crowded custom-silicon unwind that also hit Marvell (-10%). Follows June 5's ~9.5% drop on Nvidia's RTX Spark AI-PC challenge to Snapdragon X and a broad chip selloff. Investor Day June 24 (datacenter / Physical AI roadmap) is the next catalyst. YTD now +15.5% after a roughly -16% two-day drawdown.

sources.label: 24/7 Wall St - Qualcomm Drops 8% on ByteDance ASIC Deal, Marvell Falls 10%
StockStory via Yahoo Finance - Why Qualcomm (QCOM) Stock Is Nosediving (June 5 context)
TIKR - Qualcomm drops on Nvidia AI PC news; automotive $6B run rate; Investor Day June 24

verdicts.rankRationale: Genuinely new evidence since yesterday's down-move: the ByteDance custom ASIC engagement advancing makes QCOM's AI-compute exposure more direct than the 'selective edge/power' thesis assumed, and the -8% flow-driven sell-off (custom-silicon unwind, not a thesis break) restores asymmetry at rank 44.
verdicts.bull: A confirmed datacenter custom-ASIC engagement with a hyperscale-class customer plus first custom DC chip shipments this calendar year upgrades QCOM from edge-adjacent to a real AI-compute node; June 24 Investor Day can put numbers on it after a -16% two-day reset.
verdicts.neutral: The deal is real but unquantified, and export-control review could shrink or delay it; handset revenue still fell 13% YoY, so the AI node weight stays modest until DC revenue shows up.
verdicts.bear: Washington blocks or guts advanced AI silicon for a ByteDance-linked program, the AI-PC franchise loses ground to Nvidia's RTX Spark, and QCOM reverts to a melting-handset story with no bottleneck relevance.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 24 Investor Day quantifying the datacenter/custom-silicon ramp and the ByteDance engagement.
verdicts.primaryRisk: US export controls on advanced AI chips for a Chinese customer nullifying the ASIC deal.

verdicts.rankRationale: The ByteDance ASIC news is a datacenter custom-silicon story, not a CPO/optics/packaging/test bottleneck story, so yesterday's removal case on this page stands; but today's -8% de-crowding removes the chase risk, so no further down-move is warranted at rank 84.
verdicts.bull: If the custom-silicon ramp is real, QCOM's DC chips would eventually need advanced packaging and optical attach, giving it back-door relevance to the CPO chain at a now cheaper entry.
verdicts.neutral: AI-edge optionality persists, but nothing since June 6 connects QCOM to the CPO cable/optics bottleneck this page ranks; it remains a peripheral holding near the bottom of the list.
verdicts.bear: China export-control risk crystallizes exactly the 'handset cycle and China exposure' risk already flagged, while cleaner CPO names keep compounding design wins QCOM cannot match.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Investor Day (June 24) disclosing datacenter silicon volumes that would imply packaging/optics pull-through.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Zero direct CPO relevance plus China regulatory overhang keeps it dead money on this page's lens.
